expanded_summary: Research teams have independently developed hierarchical memory systems that partition agent memory into working, episodic, and semantic tiers. These architectures address the fundamental challenge of maintaining coherent context over thousands of agent steps.
why_included: Agent memory systems are a core interest matching the profile's agents and memory-systems interests
Multiple independent sources reported on the same architectural pattern
what_is_new: Tiered memory separation is now validated at scale
Novel retrieval mechanisms outperform flat context window approaches
uncertainties: Unclear how these systems perform on truly novel task domains
Computational overhead of hierarchical retrieval is not well characterized
